observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management furnish the
Auditor copies of the contract documents and
POs not later than five (5) working days from
its perfection, together with its duly
accomplished supporting documents, for
technical review and evaluation in compliance
with COA Circular No. 2009-001.

We recommend that the LCE, through the HRM Officer:
15. Exert the necessary actions to select and hire a
Municipal Accountant who can meet the
qualification standards mandated under Section
474 of R.A. 7160 so that the accounting and
internal audit functions of the Municipality be
effectively acted upon and that necessary
reports be submitted on time; and
16. Strictly observe the rules/requirements on
Human Resource Action particularly the
Designation procedure as provided in Section
13, Rule IV of the 2017 Omnibus Rules on
Appointment and Other Human Resource
Actions.

1.9 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
a) Direct the Municipal Engineer to provide the Municipal
Accountant and the General Services Officer with the complete
description and cost segregation of road components of their road
projects;
b) Direct the General Services Officer to maintain the Local Road
Network Property Card for all roads and its components; and
c) Require the Municipal Accountant the following reporting
procedures:
i) Prepare JEV to record the beginning balance of the local road
network and its components in the general ledger and the
Local Road Network Ledger Card, respectively. Support the JEV
with the Registry of Public Infrastructures for Roads, working
paper on the distribution of costs for the road components, and
working paper on the determination of the depreciated replacement
cost for road components with no available cost per registry;
ii) Keep and maintain subsidiary records for roads and road
components for every road network; and
iii) Prepare a lapsing schedule of records for the computation of
the depreciation for each component at the end of the year.

2.8 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Acting
Municipal Treasurer to:
a) Stop the practice of using cash advance to pay for the
Municipality’s operating expenses and activities;
b) Ensure that payments for suppliers are made thru checks;
c) Ensure that the purposes of cash advances are clearly stated on the
face of the disbursement vouchers and that these are duly supported
with complete documents at the time of grant of cash advances; and
d) Discontinue the practice of granting multiple cash advances.

3.12 We recommended to the Local Chief Executive the following:
a) Direct the concerned Municipality personnel, in coordination
with the BAC, to meticulously and judiciously plan the
Municipality's Annual Procurement to avoid inconvenience and
significant delays of the implementation of the Municipality’s
Project, Program and Activities thereby improving the
Municipality government’s delivery of public service;
b) Direct the BAC Secretariat to fully utilize the PhilGEPS via
posting the necessary Invitation to Bid, Notice of Award,
Perfected Contract and Notice to Proceed for each of the
Municipality's procurement to promote transparency and
efficiency;
c) Direct the BAC Secretariat to meet its function and responsibility
as the main support unit of the BAC for efficient processing of the
Municipality’s procurement;
d) Direct the Technical Working Group to properly evaluate the
financial, technical and legal capability of the bidders to provide
the HOPE with an appropriate recommendation of determining a
qualified supplier, contractor or consultant;
e) Direct the BAC to properly and completely fill out the documents
related to proçurement and to avoid tampering of such to shed out
doubt as to authenticity of the said documents: and
f) Direct the BAC and all the Municipality personnel to strictly
adhere to the provisions of RA 9184 and its 2016 IRR for the
Municipality to meet the principles of transparency,
accountability, equity, efficiency and economy in its procurement
process.

4.9 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the General
Services Officer to the following courses of actions:
a) Strictly follow the perpetual inventory method in recording and
accounting of inventories for distribution;
b) Prepare and maintain the required forms, subsidiary records and
periodic reports in relation to receipt and issuance of inventories
for distribution; and
c) Submit complete AIR’S and RISs (if necessary) as required to
support pertinent disbursement vouchers.

5.5 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the following:
a) Use of required format of sequentially numbered trip ticket duly
approved by the Agency Head or the authorized representative
for proper monitoring of fuel consumption with complete
information being filled out in every detail needed to support the
claim for payment of fuel expenses;
b) Enforce the “No Driver’s Trip Ticket, No Withdrawal Policy;” and
c) Install controls on the monitoring of issuance of the fuel and
strictly adhere to the guidelines on the use of government vehicles.

6.5 We recommended the following that the Local Chief Executive:
a) Require the GSO to:
i. Maintain Property Cards (PC) for the PPEs to account for
the receipt and (disposition; and
ii. Ensure that the deliveries of properties and equipment are
immediately recorded in the Property Cards (PC) based on
the AIR and other supporting documents;
b) Direct the Municipal Accountant to:
i. Maintain ledger cards for each category of PPEs;
ii. Make a reconciliation between the inventory reports,
property cards, and subsidiary ledgers;
iii. Ensure that the deliveries of properties and equipment are
immediately recorded in the PPE Subsidiary Ledger Cards
based on the AIR and other supporting documents; and
ensure strict adherence to the above-stated laws, rules, and
regulations.

7.5 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the
Inspectorate Team/Committee to furnish the COA Audit Team copies
of AIRs within 24 hours after the acceptance and inspection of
deliveries so that timely inspection thereof can be conducted by the
Auditors.

We recommended that the
concerned local officials
and accountable officers to
submit the following
accounting records, reports
and forms to the Auditor
within the reglementary
period as prescribed by the
applicable COA
rules and regulations, to wit:
a. Local Accountant
1. Bank Reconciliation
Statements – within 10
days from receipts of the
Bank Statements
furnished by the LGU
depository; and
2. Report of Checks Issued,
Report of
Disbursements, and
Report of Collections
and Deposits –
immediately after
recorded in the books of
accounts, within 10 days
of the ensuing month;
3. Pre-closing Trial Balance
– not later than the
(20th) day after the end
of the month;
4. Post-closing Trial
Balance – not later than
the (14th) day of February
after the end of the
calendar year; and
5. Year-end Financial
Statements – within 60
days after 31st of
December of each year;
6. For local Treasurer,
consolidated Report of
Accountability for
Accountable Forms
(CRAAF) – not later than
the fifth (5th) day of the
ensuing month; and
7. For local DRRM Officer,
report on Sources and
Utilization of Disaster
Risk Reduction and
Management Fund
(DRRMF) – on or before
15th day after the end of
each month.
8. We recommended
further, that the Local
Chief Executive require
the authorized
government depository
bank to furnish a
monthly bank statement
reports on the condition
of the LGU account
standing on their books
in pursuant to Section 74
of PD 1445.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and
the Municipal Treasurer:
22. Submit Report of Disbursement as prescribed under
Section 54 Volume II of the New Government
Accounting System (NGAS) Manual for Local
Government Units (LGUs).

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer
deposit/remit the collection at least once a week or as
soon as the collections reach ₱5,000.00 as required
under Section 29 of the NGAS Volume I.
We also recommended that the Municipal Treasurer
prepare monthly Report of Collections and Deposits,
submit the same to COA and exercise due diligence in
the supervision over collecting officers under his
supervision.

25. We recommended that the Local Chief
Executive hire/appoint a General Services Officer
who shall take custody of and be accountable for all
PPE properties, real or personal, owned by the
Municipality.
26. We recommended that Management create
Inventory Committee to once and for all conduct
complete physical count of all properties owned
by the Municipality and prepare a report thereon
in accordance with Section 156 COA Circular No. 92-386 to
determine the existence and accuracy of PPE.
27. Further, we recommended that the LCE direct the
Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer
maintain PPE Ledger Cards and Acknowledgement
Receipt for Equipment (ARE), respectively in
compliance to Section 114 of same Manual.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor
direct the Municipal Accountant to strictly
implement the usual internal control of complete
supporting documentation per COA Circular No.
2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 before any payment is
made and require all Municipal officials concerned
to strictly comply with the said internal control in
place.

We recommended that:
29. The Municipality of Tubaran employees should require all its
employees to file their membership to PAG-IBIG;
30. The Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer should
deduct the mandatory PAG-IBIG contribution and promptly remit
monthly contributions together with Agency share to the said Fund.

2. Direct the General Services Officer (Municipal Treasurer) to maintain the Local Road Network Property Card for all roads and its components; and

1.12. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the concerned
local officials and accountable officers to submit the following
accounting records, reports and forms to the Auditor within the
reglementary period as prescribed by the pertinent applicable COA
rules and regulations, to wit:
1.12.1 Local Accountant
a) Bank Reconciliation Statements – within 10 days from
receipts of the Bank Statements furnished by the LGU
depository; and
b) Report of Checks Issued, Report of Disbursements, and
Report of Collections and Deposits – immediately after
recorded in the books of accounts, within 10 days of the
ensuing month;
c) Pre-closing Trial Balance – not later than the (20th) day
after the end of the month;
d) Post-closing Trial Balance – not later than the (14th) day of
February after the end of the calendar year; and
e) Year-end Financial Statements – within 60 days after 31st
of December of each year;
1.12.2 For local Treasurer, consolidated Report of Accountability for
Accountable Forms (CRAAF) – not later than the fifth (5th) day
of the ensuing month; and
1.12.3 For local DRRM Officer, report on Sources and Utilization of
Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund (DRRMF) – on
or before 15th day after the end of each month.

1.13. We recommended further, that the Local Chief Executive require the
authorized government depository bank to furnish a monthly bank
statement reports on the condition of the LGU account standing on
their books in pursuant to Section 74 of PD 1445.

2.9 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
a) Direct the Municipal Engineer to provide the Municipal
Accountant and the General Services Officer with the complete
description and cost segregation of road components of their road
projects;
b) Direct the General Services Officer to maintain the Local Road
Network Property Card for all roads and its components; and
c) Require the Municipal Accountant the following reporting
procedures:
i. Prepare JEV to record the beginning balance of the local
road network and its components in the general ledger and
the Local Road Network Ledger Card, respectively.
Support the JEV with the Registry of Public Infrastructures
for Roads, working paper on the distribution of costs for the
road components, and working paper on the determination
of the depreciated replacement cost for road components
with no available cost per registry;
ii. Keep and maintain subsidiary records for roads and road
components for every road network; and
iii. Prepare a lapsing schedule of records for the computation of
the depreciation for each component at the end of the year.

3.5 We recommended that Management furnish the Auditor copies of the
contract documents and POs not later than five (5) working days from
its perfection, together with its duly accomplished supporting
documents, for technical review and evaluation in compliance with
COA Circular No. 2009-001.

4.6 We recommended that the LCE, through the HRM Officer:
a) Exert the necessary actions to select and hire a Municipal
Accountant who can meet the qualification standards mandated
under Section 474 of R.A. 7160so that the accounting and internal
audit functions of the Municipality be effectively acted upon and
that necessary reports be submitted on time; and
b) Strictly observe the rules/requirements on Human Resource
Action particularly the Designation procedure as provided in
Section 13, Rule IV of the 2017 Omnibus Rules on Appointment
and Other Human Resource Actions.

6. We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Treasurer to record cancelled checks in the cash book and maintain Check Register where check claimants will be required to acknowledge receipt thereof in compliance to Section 41 and 42 of NGAs.
7. Prepare the Report of Disbursement for each cash advance drawn and attach the corresponding supporting payroll/paid vouchers;
8. Submit Report of Disbursement as prescribed under Section 54 Volume II of the New Government Accounting System (NGAS) Manual for Local Government Units (LGUs).
9. We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer deposit/remit the collection at least once a week or as soon as the collections reach ₱ 5,000.00 as required to Section 29 of NGAS Volume I.

10. We also recommended that the Municipal Treasurer prepare monthly Report of Collections and Deposits, submit the same to COA and exercise due diligence in the supervision over collecting officers under his supervision.

14. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to strictly implement the usual internal control of complete supporting documentation per COA Circular No. 2012- 001 dated June 14, 2012 before any payment is made and require all Municipal officials concerned to strictly comply with the said internal control in place.

15. We recommended that the LGU should observe prescribed budgeting procedures. Specifically, appropriate the 20% EDF in accordance the prescribed projects and programs per DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2010-138 and DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 1 series of 2005 dated September 20, 2005.

1. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive hire/appoint a General Services Officer who shall take custody of and be accountable for all properties, real or personal, owned by the Municipality.
2. We also recommended that Management create Inventory Committee to once and for all conduct complete physical count of all properties owned by the Municipality and prepare a report thereon in accordance with Section 156 COA Circular No. 92-386 to determine the existence and accuracy of PPE.

10. The Municipality of Tubaran employees should require all its employees to file their membership to PAG-IBIG;
11. The Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer should deduct the mandatory PAG-IBIG contribution and promptly remit monthly contributions together with Agency share to the said Fund.

1.9 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:

a.) Direct the Municipal Engineer to provide the Municipal Accountant
and the General Services Officer with the complete description and
cost segregation of road components of their road projects;

b.) Direct the General Services Officer (Municipal Treasurer) to
maintain the Local Road Network Property Card for all roads and its
components; and

c.) Require the Municipal Accountant the following reporting
procedures:

i. Prepare JEV to record the beginning balance of the local road
network and its components in the general ledger and the Local
Road Network Ledger Card, respectively. Support the JEV
with the Registry of Public Infrastructures for Roads, working
paper on the distribution of costs for the road components,
and working paper on the determination of the depreciated
replacement cost for road components with no available cost per
registry;
ii. Keep and maintain subsidiary records for roads and road
components for every road network; and
iii. Prepare a lapsing schedule records for the computation of the
depreciation for each component at the end of the year.

2.5 We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Treasurer to
record cancelled checks in the cash book and maintain Check Register
where check claimants will be required to acknowledge receipt thereof in
compliance to Sections 41 and 42, Volume I of NGAs Manual for LGUs.

3.4 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal
Treasurer:

a. Prepare the Report of Disbursement for each cash advance drawn
and attach the corresponding supporting payroll/paid vouchers;
and

b. Submit Report of Disbursement as prescribed under of Section 54,
Volume II of the New Government Accounting System (NGAS)
Manual for Local Government Units (LGUs).

4.4 We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer:

a. Deposit/remit the collection at least once a week or as soon as the
collections reach ₱5,000.00 as required under Section 29, Volume I of
NGAS Manual for LGUs.

b. Prepare monthly Report of Collections and Deposits, submit the same
to Office of the Auditor and exercise due diligence in the supervision
over collecting officers under his supervision.

3. We recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer must prepare and maintain Registries of Appropriation and Obligations (RAO) for Personal Services (PS), Maintenance and Other Operating Expenses (MOOE), Capital Outlay (CO) and, Financial Expenses (FE);
4. We recommended that the Management should justify the lapses noted and justify the bases of the preparation of the financial statements.

5.We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to strictly implement the usual internal control of complete supporting documentation per COA Circular No. 2012- 001 dated June 14, 2012 before any payment is made and require all Municipal officials concerned to strictly comply with the said internal control in place.

6. We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to strictly observe the prescribed time to submit these documents to COA and for the immediate supervisors to monitor its compliance.
7. We also recommended that Management require all the concerned officials to submit immediately all unsubmitted documents, reports, ORs, liquidations and the like.

8. We recommended that the LGU should observe prescribed budgeting procedures. Specifically, appropriate the 20% EDF in accordance with the prescribed projects and programs per DILG Memorandum Circular No. series of 2005dated September 20, 2005.

9. We recommended that Management require the Accounting Department to prepare separate trial balances for the special accounts in the General Fund most importantly for development projects funded out of the IRA that will facilitate reporting of funds utilized and review thereof.

We recommended that Management create Inventory Committee to conduct complete physical count of all properties owned by the Municipality and prepare a report thereon in accordance with Section 124 and 114 of NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume I to determine the existence and accuracy of PPE.

We recommended that the LGU should observe prescribed budgeting procedures. Specifically, appropriate the 20% EDF in accordance with the prescribed projects and programs per DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2010-138 and DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 1 series of 2005 dated September 20, 2005.

We recommended that Management require the Accounting Department to prepare separate trial balances for the special accounts in the General Fund most importantly for development projects funded out of the IRA that will facilitate reporting of funds utilized and review thereof.

We recommended that Management strictly and consistently apply the pertinent provisions of BIR RR No. 16-2005, as amended, and BIR RR 2-98, as amended, concerning their responsibility of withholding tax as withholding agent of the government. Otherwise, the amount of tax not withheld including penalties shall be charged to the persons responsible for the withholding and remittance of the tax, as provided under Section 251 of RA No. 8424, otherwise known as the Tax Reform Act of 1997.

1.8 We recommended that:
a) The Local Chief Executive hire/appoint a General Services Officer
who shall take custody of and be accountable for all properties, real
or personal, owned by the Municipality.
b) The management create an Inventory Committee to once and for all
conduct a complete physical count of all properties owned by the
Municipality and prepare a report pursuant to Section 156 COA
Circular No. 92-386 to determine the existence and accuracy of PPE.

2.5 We recommended that Management strictly and consistently apply the
pertinent provisions of BIR RR No. 16-2005, as amended, and BIR RR
2-98, as amended, concerning their responsibility of withholding tax as
withholding agent of the government. Otherwise, the amount of tax not
withheld including penalties shall be charged to the persons responsible
for the withholding and remittance of the tax, as provided under Section
251 of RA No. 8424, otherwise known as the Tax Reform Act of 1997.

3.3 We recommended that:
a) The Municipal Budget Officer must prepare and maintain
Registries of Appropriation and Obligations (RAO) for personal
services (PS), maintenance and other operating expenses
(MOOE), capital outlay (CO) and, financial expenses (FE); and
b) The management should justify the lapses noted and justify the
bases of the preparation of the financial statements.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to transfer/deposit the unexpended balance to Special Trust Fund under the account “Trust Liability- DRRM” in the Trust Fund Books” pursuant to COA Circular 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012 which prescribes the accounting and reporting guidelines for LDRRMF of the Local Government Units, National Local Risk Reduction Management Fund given to LGUs.

Further, we recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer maintain PPE Ledger Cards and Acknowledgement Receipt for Equipment (ARE), respectively in compliance to Section 114 of same Manual.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to strictly implement the usual internal control of complete supporting documentation per COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 before any payment is made and require all Municipal officials concerned to strictly comply with the said internal control in place.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to strictly observe the prescribed time to submit these documents to COA and for the immediate supervisors to monitor its compliance.
We also recommended that Management require all the concerned officials to submit immediately all unsubmitted documents, reports, ORs, liquidations and the like.

We recommended that Management require the Accountable Officer to follow up the approved fidelity bond from the Office of the Treasury, and to submit to the Office of the Auditor the secured approved and confirmed fidelity bond in compliant to Treasury Circular 2009-002 and other laws and rules.
We reiterated to recommend that the LGU formulate LDPC and LIPC and utilize and implement LCPC Fund in accordance with identified PPAs as provided in DILG MC No. 2012-120 dated July 4, 2012.
We reiterated to recommend that the LGU formulate LDPC and LIPC and utilize and implement LCPC Fund in accordance with identified PPAs as provided in DILG MC No. 2012-120 dated July 4, 2012.

We recommended that Management conduct orientation among concerned personnel on GAD in particular to the provisions of PCW-NEDA-DBM JC 2012-10. Specifically, make sure that proper budgeting, accounting and reporting of GAD Fund are duly complied with and, that, the Fund can only be utilized according to the intended purpose(s).

13.6. Likewise, for proper implementation of the foregoing regulations,
Management is enjoined to observe the following computation of

14.5. We recommended that Management conduct orientation among
concerned personnel on GAD in particular to the provisions of
PCW-NEDA-DBM JC 2012-01. Specifically, make sure that proper
budgeting, accounting and reporting of GAD Fund are duly complied
with and, that, the Fund can only be utilized according to the intended
purpose(s).

15.5. We recommended that Management exhaust all available remedies
provided under existing laws, rules and regulations in enforcing
immediate compliance with the audit suspensions pursuant to Section
7.1.1 of COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit the BRS and its supporting documents monthly to the Auditor, within ten days after receipt of the Bank Statements in compliance with the Section 74 of PD 1445 and COA Circular 96-011 dated October 2, 1996.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to transfer or deposit the unexpended balance to Special Trust Fund under the account “Trust Liability-DRRM” in the Trust Fund Books” pursuant to COA Circular 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012 which prescribes the accounting and reporting guidelines for LDRRMF of the Local Government Units, National Local Risk Reduction Management Fund given to LGUs.

We recommended that the agency should observe prescribed budgeting procedures. Specifically, appropriate the 20% EDF in accordance with the prescribed projects and programs per DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2010-138 and DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 1 series of 2005 dated September 20, 2005.

We recommended that Management a. Prepare LDRRMFIP for the DRRM program annually in the prescribed format required under COA Circular No. 2012-002; and b. Direct the Municipal Accountant and MDRRMO to submit Monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF in prescribed format on or before the 15th day after the end of each month.

We recommended that Management strictly implement the mandatory deductions pertaining to GSIS, Philhealth and withholding tax for personnel and remitting said deductions on time in compliance with the above-cited provisions of laws and regulations.

We recommended that: a. The Municipality of Tubaran should require all its employees to file their membership to PAG-IBIG; b. The Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer should deduct the mandatory PAG-IBIG contribution and promptly remit monthly contributions together with Agency share to the said Fund.
We have recommended to the Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit monthly bank reconciliation statements regularly for trust funds pursuant to Section 74 of PD 1445 and COA Cir. 96-11.

We recommend that the agency should observe prescribed budgeting procedures. Specifically, appropriate the 20% EDF in accordance with the prescribed projects and programs per DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2010-138 and DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 1 series of 2005 dated September 20, 2005.

We recommended that the unexpended balance be transferred/deposited to Special Trust Fund under the account “Trust Liability-DRRM” in the Trust Fund Books” pursuant to COA Circular 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit the BRS and its supporting documents monthly to the Auditor, within ten days after receipt of the Bank Statements in compliance with the Section 74 of PD 1445 and COA Circular 96-011 dated October 2, 1996.

We recommended that Management create Inventory Committee to conduct complete physical inventory of all properties owned by the Municipality and prepare a report thereon in accordance with Section 124 and 114 of NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume I to determine the existence and accuracy of PPE.
We further recommended that the LCE directs the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to maintain PPE Ledger Cards and Acknowledgement Receipt for Equipment (ARE), respectively in compliance to Section 114 of same Manual.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to transfer or deposit the unexpended balance to Special Trust Fund under the account “Trust Liability-DRRM” in the Trust Fund Books” pursuant to COA Circular 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012 which prescribes the accounting and reporting guidelines for LDRRMF of the Local Government Units, National Local Risk Reduction Management Fund given to LGUs.

We recommended that management should initiate necessary reforms for fiscal reporting and accountability by: Training personnel to gain work competence and or hiring additional qualified personnel and or restructuring existing personnel giving the right job for the right person; Enforcing due compliance with laws, rules and regulations on the rendition of financial reports. It is further recommended that LCE should direct the concerned municipal officials to submit promptly the period financial reports together with the pertinent supporting documents to COA for timely post audit of transaction and communication the results thereof.

We recommended that management particularly to the members of the Bids and Awards Committee to strictly adhere to the above mentioned regulations. In cases of procurement method applied not within approved APP, the BAC through a resolution must recommend to the Head of the Procuring Entity the specific alternative method of procurement to be used and secure the prior approval pursuant to 3.2 Section 48.1 and 48.3 of IRR of RA 9184.

Recommendations: Require the completion of the physical inventory report and determine the insurable properties; Instruct the General Services Officer to officially request the GSIS to conduct evaluation of properties for the consideration in the insurance plan coverage; Direct the General Services Officer to coordinate with the Municipal Budget Officer in the estimation of premiums that may be incurred for the insurance plan; and Advise the Municipal Budget Officer to appropriate the necessary amount to cover for the payment of premiums for the insurance of the properties in annual budget. We further recommended strict compliance with R.A. No. 656 (Property Insurance Law) to ensure that all identified insurable assets belonging to the Municipality of Tubaran are adequately covered /insured with GIF of the GSIS.

We recommended that the Municipal Planning and Development Officer and other responsible officials be directed to submit GAD Accomplishment Report to the Auditor of the programs, project, and activities undertaken during the year.
1.3	We have recommended to the Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit monthly bank reconciliation statements regularly for trust funds pursuant to Section 74 of PD 1445 and COA Cir. 96-11.

2.8  We recommended that management should initiate necessary reforms for fiscal reporting and accountability by:
Training personnel to gain work competence and or hiring additional qualified personnel and or restructuring existing personnel giving the right job for the right person;
Enforcing due compliance with laws, rules and regulations on the rendition of financial reports.
2.9  It is further recommended that LCE should direct the concerned municipal officials to submit promptly  the period financial reports together with the pertinent supporting documents to COA for timely post audit of transaction and communication the results thereof.

3.5  We recommend that management particularly to the members of the Bids and Awards Committee to strictly adhere to the above mentioned regulations.  In cases of procurement method applied not within approved APP, the BAC through a resolution must recommend to the Head of the Procuring Entity the specific alternative method of procurement to be used and secure the prior approval pursuant to 3.2  Section 48.1 and 48.3 of IRR of RA 9184.:

4.7 We recommend that the agency should observe prescribed budgeting procedures.  Specifically, appropriate the 20% EDF in accordance with the prescribed projects and programs per DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2010-138 and DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 1 series of 2005 dated September 20, 2005.

We recommend that the Municipal Planning and Development Officer and other responsible officials be directed to submit GAD Accomplishment Report to the Auditor of the programs, project, and activities undertaken during the year.

We recommend that the unexpended balance be transferred/deposited to Special Trust Fund under the account “Trust Liability-DRRM” in the Trust Fund Books” pursuant to COA Circular 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.

activity;

the settlement of any
payables to the GSIS
as agreed in the
MOA.
